name: D.Gallerie
suggested_pitch: Adding a consultation booking form and a visible phone number could convert more of your premium traffic.
message_email:
Subject: Quick wins for dgallerie.sa

Hi,

I came across D.Gallerie while researching premium furniture showrooms in Riyadh. Your site stands out — the interior photography and overall aesthetic are genuinely high-end.

Two areas that could help capture more leads: there's no consultation booking form for visitors who want design services, and your phone number isn't visible without digging into the contact page. Given the premium audience you're attracting, a short booking form on the homepage and a phone number in the header could turn more browsers into consultations.

Would you be open to a free 15-minute walkthrough of these quick improvements?

Best regards
message_whatsapp: Hi — I looked at dgallerie.sa and the design is genuinely premium. Two quick observations: there's no consultation booking form on the homepage, and the phone number isn't visible in the header. Both small additions could help convert more of your high-end traffic into showroom visits. Happy to share specifics if useful.

name: Miro Furniture
suggested_pitch: Softening the color palette and adding a contact form could help premium buyers feel more welcome.
message_email:
Subject: Quick wins for miro-sa.com

Hi,

I came across Miro Furniture while looking at furniture stores in Riyadh. Your tiered discount system is clever and your category navigation makes browsing easy.

Two things that could sharpen the experience: the bold red and gray graphics dominate the homepage so strongly that the actual product images get overshadowed. Also, there's no contact or enquiry form on the homepage — visitors who want to ask a question before buying have to navigate away.

A calmer visual balance and a simple enquiry form could help convert more visitors.

Would a free 15-minute walkthrough be helpful?

Best regards
message_whatsapp: Hi — I checked out miro-sa.com and your tiered discount system is smart. Two things that could help: the bold red/gray sale graphics overshadow your product images, and there's no enquiry form on the homepage. Toning down the graphics and adding a form could boost conversions. Happy to walk through it if useful.

name: In House
suggested_pitch: A WhatsApp button and a simpler web CTA could capture visitors who don't want to download an app first.
message_email:
Subject: Quick wins for inhouse.sa

Hi,

I came across In House while researching furniture retailers in Riyadh. Your product photography and room-based browsing are well done, and the Arabic-first approach clearly fits the market.

Two areas that might be costing you web-based enquiries: there's no WhatsApp or chat button for visitors who want instant contact, and the prominent app download CTA may be losing visitors who just want to browse and buy on the web.

A floating WhatsApp button and a simpler web CTA could capture more visitors who aren't ready to install an app.

Would you be open to a free 15-minute walkthrough?

Best regards
message_linkedin: Hi — I looked at inhouse.sa and your product range and Arabic-first design are strong. Two quick observations: there's no WhatsApp or chat button for instant contact, and the app download CTA may be losing web-only visitors. A floating WhatsApp button could capture more enquiries without requiring an app install. Happy to share specifics in a quick call.
